findings.title: Shell command injection in worker disk usage helper
findings.impact: If an operator-supplied or config-derived data_dir contains shell metacharacters such as ';', '$()', or backticks, this helper executes those tokens as part of the shell command. Any caller that passes untrusted worker directory input can turn a disk-usage check into arbitrary command execution with the dscli process privileges.
findings.fix: Avoid the shell and pass arguments as a list, for example subprocess.run(["du", "-sh", data_dir], shell=False, ...). If the path can come from external input, also validate or resolve it against the allowed worker data-directory roots before invoking du.
